justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model with independent EU/US/APAC cells where no raw data crosses regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency explicitly addressed. Global dashboard uses anonymized event streams via PII-stripping bridge, never queries regional databases. Courier assignment geo-fence enforced in Assignment aggregate. Trade-offs extensively cover 3-region operational overhead (3x infrastructure, monitoring, deployment pipelines). Degraded EU service designed with cached reads and write queuing, explicitly rejecting cross-region failover.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough cell-based architecture with clear PII firewall at region boundaries
Global dashboard correctly designed as CQRS read model consuming only anonymized/aggregated events
Geo-fence invariant enforced at both domain (Assignment aggregate) and gateway levels as defense-in-depth
Degraded mode explicitly rejects cross-region failover as GDPR-violating, provides cached reads + write queue
Trade-offs include schema migration coordination, operational complexity, and feature parity challenges across 3 regions
Risks well-identified including schema drift, degraded mode staleness, and geo-fence bypass through internal APIs
Phased implementation guidance is practical and each phase is independently shippable

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform architecture expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC. The design uses region-sovereign data planes for GDPR/CCPA compliance, a lightweight global control plane for cross-region coordination, and an asynchronous event mesh for the global operations dashboard. Each region is a self-contained deployment that owns its customer and courier data, enforces geo-fencing at the domain level, and can degrade gracefully during outages. The glob
